title: bridge-slack: text message body from Slack has no size cap before nie pad call
description: bridge-slack/src/bridge.rs: Slack message text is extracted from inner.text_body() (which returns the raw text field from SlackInnerEvent), formatted via format_for_nie(), sent on the mpsc channel, and then pad() is called on the serialized ClearMessage. The Slack Events API has a body limit (bridge enforces 1MB via DefaultBodyLimit), but a single message text field could still be hundreds of KB. There is no per-message text size check before the pad() call. pad() will return Err and the message is silently dropped with a warn log. Scenario: Slack message with very long text body (e.g. pasted code block) is silently dropped at the nie relay boundary with no user-visible feedback.
